---
title: "main"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-22"
---


# Part 1
1.1
```{r}

filename <- "figures/1_1_sim_5_realizations.pdf"

#Random seed
set.seed(42)

#Given parameters
n <- 100
a <- 0.9
b <- 1
sigma1 <- 1
X0 <- 5
num_realizations <- 5

#Init matrix to store states
# Need n+1 rows for values from 0 to 100
#Each column one realization
X <- matrix(0, nrow = n+1, ncol = num_realizations)

#Initial value for all realizations
X[1, ] <- X0

#Simulate 5 realizations
for (i in 1:num_realizations){
  for (t in 2:(n+1)){
    #Gaussian noise
    e_t <- rnorm(1, mean = 0, sd = sigma1)
    
    #Calculate next state based on equation
    X[t, i] <- a * X[t-1, i] + b + e_t
  }
}

#Plotting
time_steps <- 0:n
cols <- c("#E41A1C", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#984EA3", "#FF7F00")

pdf(filename, width = 9, height = 5)
par(mar = c(5,5,3,2))

matplot(time_steps, X,
        type = "l",
        lty = 1,
        lwd = 1.2,
        col = adjustcolor(cols, 0.8),
        xlab = "Time (t)",
        ylab = expression(X[t])
        )

#legend
legend("bottomright", legend = paste("Realization", 1:5),
         col = adjustcolor(cols, 0.8), lwd = 1.2, lty = 1, bty = "n", cex = 0.75)

```

1.2
```{r}
filename <- "figures/1_2_sim_latent_and_noisy.pdf"

set.seed(42)

#Given parameters
n <- 100
a <- 0.9
b <- 1
sigma1 <- 1
sigma2 <- 1
X0 <- 5

#Init vectors to store X and Y
X <- numeric(n+1)
Y <- numeric(n+1)

# Initial values
X[1] <- X0
Y[1] <- X[1] + rnorm(1, mean = 0, sd = sigma2)

#simulate
for (t in 2:(n+1)){
  e1_t <- rnorm(1, mean = 0, sd = sigma1)
  e2_t <- rnorm(1, mean = 0, sd = sigma2)
  
  #calculate next steps
  X[t] <- a * X[t-1] + b + e1_t
  Y[t] <- X[t] + e2_t
}

#plotting
time_steps <- 0:n
cols <- c("#E41A1C", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#984EA3", "#FF7F00")

pdf(filename, width = 9, height = 5)
par(mar = c(5,5,3,2))

# plot simulated latent states using lines
plot(time_steps, X,
     type = "l",
     lty = 1,
     lwd = 2,
     col = adjustcolor(cols[2], 0.9), # Blue line for the true state
     xlab = "Time (t)",
     ylab = "Value",
     ylim = range(c(X, Y)), # Ensure the y-axis scales to fit both X and Y
)

# Plot noisy obs using points
lines(time_steps, Y,
      type = "b",        # "b" for both points and lines
      lty = 2,           # 2 for a dashed line
      pch = 16,          # Solid dots
      cex = 0.8,
      col = adjustcolor(cols[1], 0.6) # Red color with transparency
)

# Legend
legend("bottomright", 
       legend = c(expression(paste("True Latent State (", X[t], ")")), 
                  expression(paste("Noisy Observations (", Y[t], ")"))),
       col = c(adjustcolor(cols[2], 0.9), adjustcolor(cols[1], 0.6)), 
       lwd = c(2, NA),   # Line width for X, NA for Y
       pch = c(NA, 16),  # No point for X, solid dot for Y
       lty = c(1, NA),   # Solid line for X, NA for Y
       bty = "n", 
       cex = 0.85)

dev.off()

message("Plot successfully saved to: ", filename)

```

1.3
given function (modified):
```{r}
myKalmanFilter <- function(
  y,             # Vector of observations y_t
  theta,       # Model parameters for X_{t+1} = a*X_t + b + e_t
  R,             # Measurement noise variance (sigma2^2)
  x_prior = 0,   # Initial prior mean for X_0
  P_prior = 10   # Initial prior variance for X_0
) {
  a <- theta[1]
  b <- theta[2]
  sigma1 <- theta[3]
  N <- length(y)
  
  x_pred  <- numeric(N)  # Predicted means: \hat{X}_{t|t-1}
  P_pred  <- numeric(N)  # Predicted variances: P_{t|t-1}
  x_filt  <- numeric(N)  # Filtered means: \hat{X}_{t|t}
  P_filt  <- numeric(N)  # Filtered variances: P_{t|t}
  innovation      <- numeric(N)  # Pre-fit residuals: Y_{t} - \hat{X}_{t|t-1}
  innovation_var  <- numeric(N)  # Innovation covariance: P_{t|t-1} + R
  
  for (t in seq_len(N)) {
    # the prediction step
    if (t == 1) {
      x_pred[t] <- a*x_prior + b     # the mean prediction using the prior
      P_pred[t] <- a^2 * P_prior + sigma1^2              # the variance prediction using the prior
    } else {
      x_pred[t] <-  a*x_filt[t-1] + b                # the mean prediction using the previous filtered estimate
      P_pred[t] <-  a^2 * P_filt[t-1] + sigma1^2                 # the variance prediction using the previous filtered estimate
    }
    
    # the update step
    innovation[t] <- y[t] - x_pred[t] # the prediction error
    innovation_var[t] <- P_pred[t] + R # the prediction error variance
    K_t <-  P_pred[t] * 1/innovation_var[t]  # the Kalman gain
    x_filt[t] <- x_pred[t] + K_t * innovation[t] # the filtered estimate
    P_filt[t] <- P_pred[t] - K_t^2 * innovation_var[t] # the filtered estimate variance
  }
  
  return(list(
    x_pred = x_pred,
    P_pred = P_pred,
    x_filt = x_filt,
    P_filt = P_filt,
    innovation = innovation,
    innovation_var = innovation_var
  ))
}

```


```{r}
# --- 1. Run the Kalman Filter ---
# use the data generated from step 1.2. 
# We drop t=0 (index 1) to match the filter's expectation of observations starting at t=1
Y_obs <- Y[2:(n+1)]
X_true <- X[2:(n+1)]
time_obs <- 1:n

# Define parameters based on 1.1/1.2
theta <- c(0.9, 1, 1) # a, b, sigma1
R_var <- 1^2          # Measurement noise variance (sigma2^2)

# Run the filter (using X0 = 5 , and  moderate prior variance)
kf_res <- myKalmanFilter(y = Y_obs, theta = theta, R = R_var, 
                         x_prior = X0, P_prior = 1)

# --- 2. Calculate the 95% Confidence Interval ---
# CI for the predicted state: \hat{X}_{t|t-1} +/- 1.96 * sqrt(P_{t|t-1})
ci_upper <- kf_res$x_pred + 1.96 * sqrt(kf_res$P_pred)
ci_lower <- kf_res$x_pred - 1.96 * sqrt(kf_res$P_pred)

# --- 3. Plotting ---
filename_1_3 <- "figures/1_3_kalman_filter.pdf"
cols <- c("#E41A1C", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#984EA3", "#FF7F00")

pdf(filename_1_3, width = 9, height = 5)
par(mar = c(5,5,3,2))

# Plot True State First (Solid Blue)
plot(time_obs, X_true,
     type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 1.5, col = adjustcolor(cols[2], 0.8), 
     xlab = "Time (t)", ylab = "Value",
     ylim = range(c(Y_obs, ci_upper, ci_lower)),
     main = "Kalman Filter: True vs. Predicted State"
)

# Add Shaded 95% Confidence Interval Polygon (Transparent Purple)
polygon(c(time_obs, rev(time_obs)), 
        c(ci_lower, rev(ci_upper)), 
        col = adjustcolor(cols[4], 0.2), 
        border = NA)

# Add Predicted State Line (Dashed Purple)
lines(time_obs, kf_res$x_pred, 
      lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = cols[4]) 

# Add Noisy Observations (Red Dots)
points(time_obs, Y_obs, 
       pch = 16, cex = 0.8, col = adjustcolor(cols[1], 0.5)) 

# Legend
legend("bottomright", 
       legend = c(expression(paste("True Latent State (", X[t], ")")), 
                  expression(paste("Noisy Observations (", Y[t], ")")),
                  expression(paste("Predicted State (", X[t~"+"~1~"|"~t], ")")),
                  "95% CI (Predicted)"),
       col = c(adjustcolor(cols[2], 0.8), 
               adjustcolor(cols[1], 0.5), 
               cols[4], 
               adjustcolor(cols[4], 0.3)),
       lwd = c(1.5, NA, 2, 8),
       pch = c(NA, 16, NA, NA),
       lty = c(1, NA, 2, 1),
       bty = "n", cex = 0.85)

dev.off()

message("Plot successfully saved to: ", filename_1_3)
```


Part 2

2.1
Load the dataset
```{r}
data <- read.csv("assignment4_2026/transformer_data.csv")
data
```

Plot data 

```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=11}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Rename columns for simplicity
colnames(data) <- c("t", "Y", "Ta", "S", "I")

# Reshape to long format
data_long <- data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Y, Ta, S, I),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "value")
png("figures/exploratory_plot.png", width = 1000, height = 1100)


ggplot(data_long, aes(x = t, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1,
             labeller = as_labeller(c(
               Y  = "Yt — Transformer Temp. (°C)",
               Ta = "Ta,t — Outdoor Air Temp. (°C)",
               S  = "Φs,t — Solar Radiation (W/m²)",
               I  = "ΦI,t — Transformer Load (kA)"
             ))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(Y = "#E63946", Ta = "#2196F3", S = "#4CAF50", I = "#FF9800")) +
  labs(title = "Time Series of All Four Variables",
       x = "Time (h)", y = "Value") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.spacing   = unit(1.2, "lines")
  )

dev.off()
```
2.2 Estimate a 1-dimensional state-space model

```{r}
kf_logLik_dt <- function(par, df) {
  # par: vector of parameters
  # df: data frame with observations and inputs as columns (Y, Ta, S, I)
  # par: Could be on the form c(A11, A12, A21, A22, B11, B12, B21, B22, Q11, Q12, Q22)
  A   <- matrix(par[1], 1, 1) # transition matrix
  B   <- matrix(c(par[2], par[3], par[4]), 1, 3) # input matrix
  Qlt <- matrix(par[5], 1, 1)  # lower-triangle of system covariance matrix
  Q   <- Qlt %*% t(Qlt) # THAT IS!!! The system covariance matrix is given by Qlt %*% t(Qlt) (and is this symmetric positive definite)
  H   <- matrix(par[6], 1, 1)     # observation matrix
  X0  <- matrix(par[7], 1, 1)     # initial state
  R_obs <- matrix(exp(par[8])^2, 1, 1) # observation noise covariance matrix
  obs_cols <- c("Y") # observation column names
  input_cols <- c("Ta","S","I") # input column names

  # pull out data
  Y  <- as.matrix(df[, obs_cols])     # m×T
  U  <- as.matrix(df[, input_cols])   # p×T
  Tn <- nrow(df)

  # init
  n      <- nrow(A)
  x_est  <- matrix(Y[1,], n, 1)            # start state from first obs
  x_est <- X0 
  P_est  <- diag(1e1, n)                   # X0 prior covariance
  logLik <- 0

  for (t in 1:Tn) {
    # prediction step
    u_t <- matrix(U[t, ], 3, 1)   # 3 x 1 input vector at time t
    
    x_pred <- A %*% x_est + B %*% u_t
    P_pred <- A %*% P_est %*% t(A) + Q

    # innovation step
    y_pred  <- H %*% x_pred 
    S_t     <- H %*% P_pred %*% t(H) + R_obs
    innov   <- matrix(Y[t, ], 1, 1) - y_pred

    # log-likelihood contribution
    logLik <- logLik - 0.5*(sum(log(2*pi*S_t)) + t(innov) %*% solve(S_t, innov))

    # update step
    K_t    <- P_pred %*% t(H) %*% solve(S_t)
    x_est  <- x_pred + K_t %*% innov
    P_est  <- (diag(n) - K_t %*% H) %*% P_pred
  }

  as.numeric(logLik)
}

# Optimizer wrapper
estimate_dt <- function(start_par, df, lower=NULL, upper=NULL) {
  negLL <- function(par) -kf_logLik_dt(par, df)
  optim(
    par    = start_par, fn = negLL,
    method = "L-BFGS-B",
    lower  = lower, upper = upper,
    control= list(maxit=1000, trace=1)
  )
}

```


```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Kalman smoother — returns filtered states & one-step-ahead residuals
# ----------------------------------------------------------
kf_filter <- function(par, df) {
  A_val     <- par[1]; B1 <- par[2]; B2 <- par[3]; B3 <- par[4]
  G_val     <- par[5]; C_val <- par[6]; log_sig_y <- par[7]
 
  A <- matrix(A_val, 1, 1)
  B <- matrix(c(B1, B2, B3), 1, 3)
  G <- matrix(G_val, 1, 1)
  C <- matrix(C_val, 1, 1)
  Q <- G %*% t(G)
  R <- matrix(exp(log_sig_y)^2, 1, 1)
 
  Y  <- as.matrix(df[, "Y",            drop = FALSE])
  U  <- as.matrix(df[, c("Ta","S","I"), drop = FALSE])
  Tn <- nrow(df)
 
  x_filt <- numeric(Tn)
  resids  <- numeric(Tn)
 
  x_est <- matrix(Y[1, ], 1, 1)
  P_est <- matrix(1e2,    1, 1)
 
  for (t in 1:Tn) {
    u_t    <- matrix(U[t, ], 3, 1)
    x_pred <- A %*% x_est + B %*% u_t
    P_pred <- A %*% P_est %*% t(A) + Q
    y_pred <- C %*% x_pred
    S_t    <- C %*% P_pred %*% t(C) + R
    innov  <- matrix(Y[t, ], 1, 1) - y_pred
    K_t    <- P_pred %*% t(C) %*% solve(S_t)
    x_est  <- x_pred + K_t %*% innov
    P_est  <- (diag(1) - K_t %*% C) %*% P_pred
 
    x_filt[t] <- as.numeric(x_est)
    resids[t]  <- as.numeric(innov)
  }
  list(x_filt = x_filt, residuals = resids)
}
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Fit the model
# ----------------------------------------------------------
 
# Load your data — adjust path / column names as needed
# Expected columns: time (or index), Y, Ta, S, I
# df <- read.csv("your_data.csv")
 
# --- Example with synthetic data if real data not yet loaded ---
set.seed(42)
Tn  <- 500
Ta  <- 10 + 5 * sin(seq(0, 4 * pi, length.out = Tn))
S   <- pmax(0, 20 * sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = Tn)))
I   <- rep(c(1, 0), each = Tn / 2) * 5
X   <- numeric(Tn); X[1] <- 20
for (t in 2:Tn) X[t] <- 0.95 * X[t-1] + 0.03 * Ta[t-1] + 0.01 * S[t-1] + 0.02 * I[t-1] + rnorm(1, 0, 0.3)
Y   <- X + rnorm(Tn, 0, 0.5)
df  <- data.frame(Y = Y, Ta = Ta, S = S, I = I)
# --- End synthetic data block ---
 
# Initial parameter guess
# par = c(A, B1_Ta, B2_solar, B3_load, Qlt, H, X0, log_R_obs)
start_par <- c(
  A         =  0.90,
  B1_Ta     =  0.05,
  B2_solar  =  0.01,
  B3_load   =  0.02,
  Qlt       =  0.50,
  H         =  1.00,
  X0        =  df$Y[1],  # initial state = first observation
  log_R_obs =  log(0.5)
)
 
lower_bounds <- c(A=0.50, B1_Ta=-0.20, B2_solar=0.00, B3_load=0.00,
                  Qlt=1e-4, H=0.80, X0=-Inf, log_R_obs=-4.0)
upper_bounds <- c(A=0.999, B1_Ta=0.50, B2_solar=0.20, B3_load=0.20,
                  Qlt=5.0,  H=1.20, X0=Inf,  log_R_obs=2.0)

 
cat("Fitting state-space model...\n")
fit <- estimate_dt(start_par, df, lower = lower_bounds, upper = upper_bounds)
 
cat("\n=== Estimated Parameters ===\n")
pnames <- c("A", "B_Ta", "B_solar", "B_load", "G", "C", "log_sigma_y")
est    <- setNames(fit$par, pnames)
print(round(est, 4))
cat("sigma_y =", round(exp(est["log_sigma_y"]), 4), "\n")
cat("Q (system noise var) =", round(est["G"]^2, 4), "\n")
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Information criteria
# ----------------------------------------------------------
logLik_val <- -fit$value
k          <- length(fit$par)
n          <- nrow(df)
AIC_val    <- -2 * logLik_val + 2 * k
BIC_val    <- -2 * logLik_val + log(n) * k
 
cat("\n=== Model Performance ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Log-likelihood : %.2f\n", logLik_val))
cat(sprintf("AIC            : %.2f\n", AIC_val))
cat(sprintf("BIC            : %.2f\n", BIC_val))
cat(sprintf("Convergence    : %d (0 = success)\n", fit$convergence))
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Diagnostic plots
# ----------------------------------------------------------
res  <- kf_filter(fit$par, df)
resids <- res$residuals
x_hat  <- res$x_filt
 
# Standardised residuals
sigma_y <- exp(est["log_sigma_y"])
std_resids <- resids / sigma_y
 
time_index <- seq_len(nrow(df))
 
png("figures/2_2.png", width = 1000, height = 500)

par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))
 

# Plot 1: Observed vs fitted
plot(time_index, df$Y, type = "l", col = "steelblue", lwd = 1.2,
     xlab = "Time", ylab = "Temperature", main = "Observed vs Filtered")
lines(time_index, x_hat, col = "firebrick", lwd = 1.5, lty = 2)
legend("topright", legend = c("Observed Y", "Filtered X"),
       col = c("steelblue","firebrick"), lty = c(1,2), bty = "n")
 
# Plot 2: Residuals over time
plot(time_index, std_resids, type = "l", col = "darkgray",
     xlab = "Time", ylab = "Standardised residual",
     main = "One-step-ahead residuals")
abline(h = c(-2, 0, 2), lty = c(2, 1, 2), col = c("red","black","red"))
 
# Plot 3: ACF of residuals
acf(std_resids, main = "ACF of residuals", lag.max = 40)
 
# Plot 4: PACF of residuals
pacf(std_resids, main = "PACF of residuals", lag.max = 40)
 
# Plot 5: QQ-plot
qqnorm(std_resids, main = "QQ-plot of residuals")
qqline(std_resids, col = "red", lwd = 2)
 
# Plot 6: Residuals vs fitted (heteroscedasticity check)
plot(x_hat, std_resids, pch = 20, col = adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.4),
     xlab = "Filtered state", ylab = "Std. residual",
     main = "Residuals vs Fitted")
abline(h = 0, col = "red")
 
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
dev.off()
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Summary table for the report
# ----------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== Parameter Interpretation ===\n")
cat(sprintf("A = %.4f  : Fraction of heat retained each time step (thermal inertia)\n", est["A"]))
cat(sprintf("           Half-life ≈ %.1f steps\n", -log(2)/log(est["A"])))
cat(sprintf("B_Ta    = %.4f : Temperature rise per 1 unit of outdoor temp\n", est["B_Ta"]))
cat(sprintf("B_solar = %.4f : Temperature rise per 1 W/m² of solar radiation\n", est["B_solar"]))
cat(sprintf("B_load  = %.4f : Temperature rise per 1 unit of internal load\n", est["B_load"]))
cat(sprintf("G       = %.4f : System noise std (model uncertainty)\n", est["G"]))
cat(sprintf("C       = %.4f : Observation gain (≈1 means direct temp measurement)\n", est["C"]))
cat(sprintf("sigma_y = %.4f : Measurement noise std\n", sigma_y))
```
